# Implementation Plan: Backend API Alignment for Frontend-2

**Branch**: `002-api-alignment` | **Date**: 2026-02-19 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/002-api-alignment/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by `/speckit.plan`. All Phase 0 and Phase 1 artifacts have been produced.

## Summary

The backend currently exposes 6 endpoints, but 3 are missing (list meetings, get meeting detail, delete meeting), 2 are broken (upload auth disabled, background task never triggered), and the JWT secret is hardcoded. This plan adds the missing endpoints, fixes the broken ones, and moves secrets to environment configuration — aligning the backend fully with what `frontend-2` requires to be a functional meeting notes application. No database schema changes are required; the `Meeting` model already stores transcript, summary, and action items.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.11 (backend), TypeScript / Node.js 20 (frontend-2, to be scaffolded)
**Primary Dependencies**: FastAPI, SQLModel, Celery (Redis broker), pydantic-ai, pypdf, python-jose
**Storage**: PostgreSQL (relational data), Redis (task queue), filesystem `/media/` (uploaded files)
**Testing**: pytest (backend), to be confirmed for frontend-2
**Target Platform**: Linux (Docker containers), local development via Docker Compose
**Project Type**: Web application — FastAPI backend + Next.js frontend (separate containers)
**Performance Goals**: Meetings list loads in < 2 seconds; meeting detail loads in < 2 seconds; file upload accepted in < 5 seconds for files up to 50 MB
**Constraints**: Stateless API (auth via JWT bearer token); file storage on shared Docker volume `/media/`; background processing via Celery (no streaming)
**Scale/Scope**: Personal / small-team tool; expected < 1,000 meetings per user; no horizontal scaling required for MVP

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

> The project constitution (`/.specify/memory/constitution.md`) has not been customized for this project — it contains only the template placeholder text. No project-specific principles or gates are defined.
>
> **Decision**: Proceeding with general software engineering best practices as the governing standard:
> - Keep changes minimal and non-breaking
> - No new dependencies without justification
> - All new endpoints must be authenticated
> - Secrets must not be hardcoded in source code

**Gate result**: PASS (no constitution violations to evaluate)

**Post-design re-check**: PASS — design introduces no new dependencies, no new services, no architectural changes. All new endpoints follow existing FastAPI patterns.

```text
backend/
├── app/
│   ├── api/

│   │   ├── upload.py       # Fix: enable auth + trigger Celery task + UUID filenames
│   │   ├── meetings.py     # NEW: list, get, delete meetings
│   │   ├── login.py        # No change

│   │   ├── styles.py       # No change
│   │   └── deps.py         # No change (get_current_user already implemented)
│   ├── core/
│   │   ├── config.py       # Add: SECRET_KEY from env var
│   │   └── security.py     # Fix: use settings.SECRET_KEY instead of hardcoded string
│   ├── models.py           # No schema change; MeetingRead already exposes all fields
│   └── main.py             # No change
└── .env.example            # Add SECRET_KEY variable

docker-compose.yml          # Update web service: ./frontend → ./frontend-2
frontend-2/                 # Scaffold new Next.js application (separate feature 001)
```

**Structure Decision**: Web application layout (backend + frontend-2). All backend changes are confined to `backend/app/`. No new packages or services introduced.

## Complexity Tracking

> No constitution violations. No complexity justification required.

---

## Phase 0: Research

*See [research.md](research.md) for full findings.*

**Questions resolved:**

| Question | Decision | Rationale |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| Pagination style for GET /meetings/ | Offset-based (`skip` + `limit`, default 20, max 100) | Personal app; < 10k records per user; simpler to implement and test |
| File collision prevention | UUID prefix on disk filename; original name stored in DB `title` | Standard pattern; zero collision risk; display name preserved |
| Celery task trigger timing | Call `.delay()` after DB commit | Ensures meeting record exists before worker reads it; no race condition |
| Delete behavior | Hard delete (DB row + file from disk) | MVP; no audit trail requirement; simplest implementation |
| Status polling | Frontend polls `GET /meetings/{id}` every 3–5 seconds | WebSockets are overkill for a personal tool; polling is standard at this scale |

---

## Phase 1: Design

### Data Model Changes

*See [data-model.md](data-model.md) for full entity definitions.*

| Entity | Change | Why |
|--------|--------|-----|
| `Meeting` | No schema change | All required fields (transcript_text, summary_text, action_items_text, status) already exist |
| `MeetingRead` | Already complete | Response schema includes transcript, summary, action items |
| `Settings` (config) | Add `SECRET_KEY: str` and `ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES: int` | JWT secret must come from environment |
| File storage | Filenames prefixed with `{uuid}_` on disk | Prevent collision when multiple users upload same filename |

### API Contract Summary

*See [contracts/](contracts/) for full OpenAPI schemas.*

**New endpoints (3):**

| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/api/v1/meetings/` | Required | List current user's meetings, paginated by `skip`/`limit` |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/meetings/{id}` | Required | Get single meeting with all AI output |
| `DELETE` | `/api/v1/meetings/{id}` | Required | Hard delete meeting + file from storage |

**Updated endpoints (1):**

| Method | Path | Changes |
|--------|------|---------|
| `POST` | `/api/v1/upload` | Enable `get_current_user`; UUID-prefix filename; call `process_meeting.delay()` after commit |

**Security fix (cross-cutting):**

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `backend/app/core/config.py` | Add `SECRET_KEY: str` field (reads from `SECRET_KEY` env var) |
| `backend/app/core/security.py` | Replace hardcoded `"your-secret-key-here"` with `settings.SECRET_KEY` |
| `backend/app/api/deps.py` | Replace hardcoded `"your-secret-key-here"` with `settings.SECRET_KEY` |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Add `SECRET_KEY` env var to `api` and `worker` services |
| `.env.example` | Document `SECRET_KEY` as a required variable |

**Unchanged endpoints (5):**

`POST /login/access-token`, `POST /users/`, `GET /users/me`, `POST /styles/upload`, `GET /styles/`
